subparts.text: A project is different from routine and regular activities. Draw a list of characteristics that distinguish the projects from other activities. 1
What does it mean when a project’s priorities are set as follows: Time - constrain, Scope - accept, and Cost- enhance? How do these priorities influence decision-making during project execution? 2
Define Project Scope and explain its key elements. How does effective scope management contribute to project success? 2
How does the discounted cash flow method address some of the limitations of the payback period in project evaluation? 1
Describe the different phases of a project life cycle. How do these phases contribute to the successful execution of a project? 1
What are the key elements of Project Communication Management? How do effective communication strategies impact project outcomes? 1

Conduct a discounted cash flow calculation to determine the NPV of the following project, assuming a required rate of return of 14%. The project will cost $100,000 but will result in cash inflows of $10,000, $20,000, $30,000, $60,000, and $30,000 in the next five years. 1

Your Company is considering two new projects proposed for improving operational efficiency. Project A will cost $240,000 to implement and is expected to have annual net cash flows of $75,000. Project B will cost $180,000 to implement and should generate annual net cash flows of $62,000. Given that the company prioritizes cash flow, determine which project is preferable using the payback period method. Justify your choice. 2
Briefly describe three different cost estimation approaches used in project management. Under what conditions would you prefer the top-down cost-estimating approach over others? 2
Develop a WBS for organizing a health camp in your college. Try to identify all of the major components and provide two levels of detail. 2
